description: Tiny client for the public Cartograph cloud registry. Wraps GET /v1/registry/stats and GET /v1/widgets/search. Configurable baseUrl and injectable fetch (so it works in browser, Node, or test environments). Throws on non-2xx; passes AbortSignal through.

library_notes.general: Single responsibility - one widget does one thing. No global state. No project-specific code, hardcoded paths, credentials, or environment-specific values. Expose variation points as constructor parameters or function arguments - no hardcoded thresholds, colors, sizes, or behavior flags. URLs and endpoints belong in examples as demonstration data, not in src/ - if a widget needs a URL to operate, it must be a parameter the caller provides. All dependencies must be declared in widget.json tech_stack.dependencies. Widgets cannot depend on other widgets - they must be fully self-contained. If you need logic from another widget, copy it into src/ directly. Examples must run and exit cleanly with no user input, no network calls, and no external services or API keys - use fake/hardcoded data. The widget's own declared dependencies are fine and will be installed by the validator before the example runs. Do not modify library_notes - it is managed by Cartograph and will be restored on checkin.
library_notes.language: No project-specific names, identifiers, or terminology anywhere in src/, tests/, or examples/ - use generic names like 'user', 'item', 'value', 'example.com'. A test that references your project's real entity names or domain is contaminated. Use vitest for all tests. Include a vitest config and a coverage provider (@vitest/coverage-v8 or @vitest/coverage-istanbul) in devDependencies - the validator enforces 80% coverage via CLI flags but does not inject test configuration. No console.log() in src/ files. Use named exports, not default exports. Test filename must match src filename exactly. Only test/expect/describe/vi are global - all other imports must be explicit. Plain JS: files use .js, examples run with node and exit cleanly. Frontend (DOM) testing: add 'happy-dom' to devDependencies and set environment: 'happy-dom' in your vitest config - the scaffold does this automatically for frontend widgets. Canvas/WebGL testing: add '@vitest/browser' and 'playwright' to devDependencies and configure the browser provider in your vitest config - Playwright must be installed on the system (run 'npx playwright install chromium'). React is not scaffolded by default. To use React: add react and react-dom to dependencies, rename files to .jsx, add @vitejs/plugin-react, @testing-library/react, jsdom, and esbuild to devDependencies, and configure your vitest config with the react plugin and jsdom environment. The validator auto-detects React in dependencies to use esbuild when running the example - test environment is entirely your vitest config's responsibility. Components must be PascalCase .jsx, hooks camelCase .js starting with 'use'. Components must render cleanly via react-dom/server renderToString with no browser APIs (window, document, localStorage) in the initial render path. Usage hints: you may optionally include examples/usage_hint.* with real integration code that requires a browser or app context. These are not executed by Cartograph.
library_notes.domain: Transformation logic must be pure and stateless - same input always produces same output, no side effects. Accept the broadest practical input type (bytes, str, file-like, path) and let the caller choose. Expose encoding, delimiter, schema, and other format details as parameters. Raise descriptive errors on malformed input rather than silently coercing or dropping data.
